title: Weekly Language Lessons Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate weekly language lessons by learning goal. Free calculator, no signup—based on FSI language study.
intro: Learning a language requires consistent practice over hundreds of hours. According to the Foreign Service Institute (FSI), here's what Spanish speakers need: Italian or Portuguese ~600 hours, English or French ~900 hours, German ~1,100 hours, Arabic or Chinese ~2,200 hours. The research is clear: consistency beats intensity. A few lessons each week work better than sporadic marathon sessions. This calculator helps you find the right weekly lesson frequency for your language learning goal.
keyTakeaway: Use this formula with platforms like iTalki or Preply. Enter your learning goal to get your recommended weekly lesson frequency.
useCases: Determine optimal weekly lesson frequency for your language learning goal
Plan your iTalki or Preply tutoring schedule
Estimate total time investment to reach language proficiency
Compare lesson frequency across different learning levels
explanation:
**Learning a language** requires hundreds of hours of focused practice. Cognitive science—from Ebbinghaus's research on memory to modern spaced repetition techniques—combined with immersion and active practice form the research-backed foundation validated by the FSI and Cambridge Institute.

## How It's Calculated

This calculator applies the proven formula based on your learning goal, checking against typical ranges and alerting you to unusual values.

## Final Notes

This is a reference guide only. For critical decisions, consult a language professional. Data is current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What does 'Maintain' mean in this calculator?
faq.a: It means keeping your existing language skills from declining. Recommended: 1 lesson per week, 60 minutes.
It's for steady progress toward better proficiency. Recommended: 2-3 lessons per week, 45-60 minutes each.
For rapid progress, ideal before exams or major language goals. Recommended: 5-7 lessons per week, 60 minutes each.

faq.q: Does teacher quality affect the results?
faq.a: Absolutely. A certified professional tutor outperforms a community tutor. Choose quality carefully—it matters as much as frequency.

faq.q: How should I prepare for my lessons?
faq.a: Create a list of topics and questions before each lesson. This maximizes time with your tutor and speeds up progress.
Yes. All Hacé Cuentas calculators are free with no registration required.
They're evidence-based guidelines using internationally validated formulas. For personalized advice, consult a language professional.

faq.q: Do you store or share my data?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We never send data to any server.
